ontology.foodex2_description: Chicken meat, breast, with skin, cooked
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT code 226529007 refers to chicken breast as a general substance; skin-on and braised preparation specificity are not captured in SNOMED CT. closest_parent is used as the most appropriate match type available.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of braised chicken breast with skin is approximately 120g, equivalent to one medium chicken breast portion as commonly served in a Singaporean meal.

nutrient_source.title: Braised chicken breast, with skin — HPB Singapore Food Composition Database (Foodo)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Core macros and micronutrients (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, dietary fibre, calcium, iron) taken directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for braised chicken breast with skin (cr_id F11010004). Saturated fat, fatty acid profile, cholesterol, potassium, B vitamins, phosphorus, magnesium, zinc, selenium, and water content adapted from USDA FoodData Central NDB 05064 (chicken breast with skin, cooked, stewed) as HPB does not separately report these fields.

gi_source.title: N/A — Braised chicken breast with skin contains negligible carbohydrates; GI not applicable
gi_source.source_match_notes: Glycemic index is not applicable for this food. Braised chicken breast with skin contains 0g carbohydrates per 100g; GI testing requires a minimum carbohydrate load and is not meaningful for protein- and fat-dominant foods. The numeric value 0 is used as a sentinel to satisfy template numeric type requirements; gi_category is set to not_applicable to signal that GI measurement is not relevant. Glycemic load per serving is 0.0 by definition.

hypertension.guidance: Braised chicken breast with skin is suitable for individuals with hypertension given its low sodium content (62mg/100g); patients should avoid adding high-sodium condiments such as soy sauce or oyster sauce during or after cooking, and should consider removing the skin to reduce saturated fat intake if cardiovascular risk is elevated.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero carbohydrates and high protein (27.4g/100g) with no glycaemic impact
type2_diabetes.guidance: Braised chicken breast with skin is appropriate for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes as it contains zero carbohydrates and zero sugar, exerts no glycaemic response, and provides 27.4g of high-quality protein per 100g to support satiety and muscle preservation; removing the skin is advisable to reduce total fat and caloric intake for weight management.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate total fat (7.42g/100g) with saturated fat estimated at 2.08g/100g
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Braised chicken breast with skin is acceptable for individuals with cardiovascular disease in moderate portions; the saturated fat content (approximately 2.1g/100g) is below the threshold of concern, but removing the skin before consumption is recommended to further reduce saturated fat and total caloric intake in line with heart-healthy dietary guidelines.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Low saturated fat and high-quality lean protein
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Braised chicken breast with skin is suitable for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis; saturated fat is estimated at approximately 2.1g/100g (well below the 5g threshold of concern), and the high protein content supports muscle maintenance; removing the skin is recommended for patients with cardiovascular comorbidities.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Braised chicken breast with skin is a cooked poultry dish prepared by slow-cooking a skin-on chicken breast in liquid — typically water or a light broth with aromatics such as ginger, garlic, and spring onion — until the meat is tender and moist. The skin retains its soft, gelatinous texture after braising, contributing additional fat and flavour compared to skinless preparations. This dish is commonly found in Singaporean Chinese home cooking and hawker-style meals, often served alongside rice or noodles. Nutritionally, it is a high-protein food providing 27.4g of protein per 100g, with moderate total fat (7.42g/100g) largely attributable to the skin. Carbohydrate content is zero, making it suitable for low-carbohydrate, ketogenic, and diabetic dietary patterns. Sodium is low at 62mg/100g in its plain braised form, though this can increase significantly if soy sauce or other condiments are added. The dish is halal-certified in most Singaporean contexts and is gluten-free in its base preparation. It is a practical source of B vitamins including niacin (9.9mg/100g) and B6 (0.6mg/100g), as well as zinc and selenium. Clinically, it is appropriate for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, and cardiovascular disease, with the primary caution being the additional saturated fat from the skin.

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F11010004) with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat, fatty acid profile, and micronutrient values supplemented from USDA FoodData Central NDB 05064 (chicken breast with skin, cooked, stewed), which is the closest approved-source equivalent. Both are approved primary sources under Project Veritas v2 source policy.
notes: Carbohydrate_g is confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo database for this braised chicken breast preparation — this food contains no carbohydrate-contributing ingredients in its base form (confirmed zero). Sugar_g is confirmed zero per HPB source (confirmed zero). Dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero as this is a pure animal protein preparation with no plant-based ingredients (naturally zero). Saturated fat (2.08g), monounsaturated fat (2.79g), polyunsaturated fat (1.56g), trans fat (0.05g), cholesterol (82mg), potassium (245mg), vitamin A (12mcg RAE), B vitamins, phosphorus, magnesium, zinc, selenium, and water content are adapted from USDA FoodData Central NDB 05064 (chicken breast with skin, cooked, stewed) as HPB does not separately report these fields; this is documented as an adapted match. vitamin_c_mg set to -1: not reported in HPB Foodo or USDA FoodData Central for this preparation. vitamin_d_mcg set to -1: not reported in HPB Foodo for this preparation. vitamin_e_mg set to -1: not reported in HPB Foodo for this preparation. Glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates (0g/100g); GI testing is not meaningful for protein- and fat-dominant foods. The numeric value 0 is used in the glycemic_index field to satisfy JSON template numeric type requirements; gi_category is set to not_applicable to signal that GI measurement is not relevant for this food. The input record description states 'Boiled chicken breast' but the food name and HPB source confirm braised preparation; the description has been corrected to reflect braising. The input glycemic_index value of 0 with confidence 0.318 from 'Embedded GI Database' has been replaced with 0 (numeric sentinel) and gi_category set to not_applicable per Veritas q4 rules for negligible-carbohydrate foods.
